channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: ICE agents raid seafood store, detain Puerto Rican US military veteran
item.description: "I asked them [the agents] what documentation they were looking for, and they said it was a license or a passport.
item.dc:creator: Ben Mitchell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: North Dakota’s overturned abortion ban won’t be in effect during appeal, court rules
item.description: BISMARCK, N.D. (AP) — North Dakota’s abortion ban will not be enforced while the state appeals an earlier decision that found it unconstitutional, the state's highest court ruled Friday. That appeal has yet to fully play out in the state Supreme Court after a judge struck down the law in September.
item.dc:creator: JACK DURA, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Washington state woman is charged in the fatal shooting of a Border Patrol agent in Vermont
item.description: A Washington state woman has been charged in the fatal shooting of a U.S. Border Patrol agent in Vermont, which happened days after authorities began watching her and a German companion who also died in the roadside shootout, the FBI said Friday.
item.dc:creator: HOLLY RAMER, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Desperate families await return of 4 female soldiers held by Hamas for 15 months
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — Hamas announced Friday that it would release four female soldiers held hostage for 15 months in Gaza, as part of an exchange for dozens of Palestinian prisoners laid out in its ceasefire agreement with Israel.
item.dc:creator: SHLOMO MOR and WAAFA SHURAFA, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Secret Service says it visited Chicago elementary school, not ICE
item.description: Chicago Public Schools officials said in a press conference Friday that Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) agents showed up at an elementary school in the city but were denied entry. But later on Friday, the Secret Service said it visited the school, not ICE.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, KRON4

item.title: Oregon couple who posed as firefighters during L.A. wildfires charged, face potential jail time
item.description: The Oregon couple who allegedly tried to sneak into a Palisades Fire evacuation zone while impersonating firefighters are facing charges and potential jail time, according to prosecutors.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, WGN 9

item.title: Target latest company to roll back DEI programs
item.description: Target has announced it is rolling back its diversity, equity and inclusion (DEI) policies, becoming the latest corporation to do so following President Donald Trump’s election.
item.dc:creator: Cheyanne M. Daniels, KRON4

item.title: The man pushed onto New York's subway tracks says he will ride again
item.description: A man who survived being shoved onto subway tracks ahead of an oncoming train in New York City says that in spite of the trauma, he eventually plans to make his way back to the train.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Marilyn Manson won't face domestic violence and sexual assault charges, DA says
item.description: "We recognize and applaud the courage and resilience of the women who came forward to make reports and share their experiences, and we thank them for their cooperation and patience with the investigation," the district attorney's office said in a news release explaining the decision not to pursue ch
item.dc:creator: Christine Samra, KRON4

item.title: What JFK assassination files are still classified? Trump’s order could bring them to light
item.description: Millions of documents related to the 1963 assassination of President John F. Kennedy in Dallas have already been made public, but President Donald Trump has ordered the release of thousands of still-classified files.
item.dc:creator: Jamie Stengle, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Oklahoma wants federal inmate transferred so he can be put to death
item.description: Oklahoma’s top prosecutor is asking the federal Bureau of Prisons to transfer an inmate to state custody so that he can be executed for killing a 77-year-old woman in 1999.
item.dc:creator: Sean Murphy, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: DHS greenlights swift deportation for migrants Biden allowed in temporarily
item.description: The acting head of the Department of Homeland Security (DHS) is allowing immigration enforcement agents to swiftly deport those who came to the U.S. under multiple pathways established under the Biden administration.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Homes swatted in California; Officers taunted through Ring camera
item.description: A man pleaded guilty to reporting false emergencies to police, then taunting officers using residences' Ring cameras.
item.dc:creator: Cameron Kiszla, NewsNation

item.title: FBI arrests suspect in killing of Vermont Border Patrol agent
item.description: The FBI announced the arrest of a suspect Friday in the killing of U.S. Border Patrol Agent David "Chris" Maland near the Vermont-Canada border.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump proposes 'getting rid of FEMA' during North Carolina visit
item.description: President Donald Trump said he was considering "getting rid of FEMA” as he visited hurricane-battered North Carolina on Friday during the first trip of his second term.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT and CHRIS MEGERIAN Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: LA Mayor Karen Bass' brother loses home in Palisades Fire
item.description: Embattled Los Angeles Mayor Karen Bass has revealed that her brother lost his Malibu home in the still-raging Palisades wildfires.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump’s Jan. 6 pardons divide House Republicans
item.description: President Trump’s decision to pardon hundreds of protesters who stormed the Capitol on Jan. 6, 2021 — including many accused of assaulting police officers — is dividing House Republicans, with some lawmakers touting the day-one decree and others criticizing it as ill-advised.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: White House says migrant deportation flights with military aircraft have begun
item.description: The Trump administration has started flying immigrants who entered the U.S. illegally out of the country using military aircraft, a White House spokesperson said Friday.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Delphi murders: Ron Logan's ex-girlfriend says he could be the killer
item.description: The ex-girlfriend of a former person of interest in the case tells NewsNation she believes he could have committed the killings.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Soriano, NewsNation

item.title: Resale prices for iPhones 'with TikTok installed' soar up to $50,000
item.description: TikTok is no longer available in the App Store as of Thursday, however there's still a way to get back on the platform – for a price.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, ABC 4

item.title: Judge to weigh genetic evidence and search warrants in University of Idaho quadruple murder case
item.description: BOISE, Idaho (AP) — Attorneys for a man charged with murder in connection with the killings of four University of Idaho students are asking a judge to throw out most of the evidence in the case because they say it all hinges on an unconstitutional genetic investigation process.
item.dc:creator: REBECCA BOONE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Disneyland may remove longtime Haunted Mansion element, Imagineer says
item.description: The creative team at Disneyland has considered removing one of the Haunted Mansion’s many “frightful delights,” according to an Imagineer.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KRON4

item.title: 6 planets to align in the night sky: Here's when
item.description: You might want to keep your eyes on the skies through next month: Six planets will align in January and February.
item.dc:creator: Cassie Buchman, KRON4

item.title: Musk mocks Nazi salute accusations with puns
item.description: Elon Musk referenced Adolf Hitler and other Nazi leaders in a social media post filled with puns early Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, Fox 5

item.title: Trump’s blizzard of orders gets pushback, questions from GOP lawmakers
item.description: President Donald Trump’s blizzard of executive orders during the first few days of his presidency has sent Republican lawmakers scrambling to make sense of what impact they’ll have on the country, and some GOP senators are already raising questions and concerns.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, KRON4

item.title: Trump's renaming plans leave mapmakers wondering what to do next
item.description: President Trump's territorial assertions sparked a round of rethinking by mapmakers and teachers, snark on social media and sarcasm by at least one other world leader.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Costco stands by its DEI policies as other companies scale back
item.description: Costco is pushing back on a shareholder proposal that urges the wholesale club operator to conduct an evaluation of any business risks posed by its diversity, equity and inclusion practices. Investors were expected to vote on the recommendation during the company's annual meeting Thursday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump's new Justice Department leadership orders a freeze on civil rights cases
item.description: Attorneys in the department's Civil Rights Division were ordered not to file any new complaints, amicus briefs or other certain court papers “until further notice," one of the memos said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump says he may withhold federal aid for Los Angeles fires
item.description: The president repeated false claims that the state's fish conservation efforts in the northern part of the state are responsible for fire hydrants running dry in urban areas.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: U.S. government sues Southwest Airlines, fines Frontier for chronically delayed flights
item.description: “Airlines have a legal obligation to ensure that their flight schedules provide travelers with realistic departure and arrival times," Transportation Secretary Pete Buttigieg said in a statement.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Former Arkansas officer arrested, charged with assault in beating of handcuffed inmate
item.description: LITTLE ROCK, Ark. (AP) — A former Arkansas police officer who was caught on video beating a handcuffed inmate in the back of his patrol car last year has been arrested and charged with aggravated assault.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Nearly half of remote workers say they'd quit before returning to office full-time
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (KRON) -- Nearly half of workers who currently work remotely at least some of the time would likely leave their jobs if ordered to return to the office full-time. At least, that's the key finding in a new survey from the Pew Research Center.
item.dc:creator: Alex Baker, KRON4

item.title: Mexico setting up tents for migrants deported by Trump
item.description: Mexico on Tuesday continued to prepare for President Donald Trump’s promised mass deportations by setting up the first of nine migrant attention centers on the border.
item.dc:creator: Julian Resendiz, Fox 5

item.title: Trump popularity higher than throughout most of first term: Poll
item.description: The poll found Trump with a 47 percent approval rating, higher than almost the entirety of his first term, though still below the majority support that most other presidents have enjoyed at the start of their administrations.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Thousands of Vornado space heaters recalled over potential 'shock and fire' hazards
item.description: Thousands of Vornado space heaters sold on Amazon have been recalled amid concerns over possible "electric shock and fire hazards," according to the company.
item.dc:creator: Emily Smith, WGN 9

item.title: 5 takeaways on Trump's executive actions on gender
item.description: President Donald Trump signed a sweeping executive order late Monday during his first hours in office recognizing only two sexes and preventing government dollars from being spent on what his administration called “gender ideology.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, Fox 5

item.title: Elephants can't pursue their release from a Colorado zoo because they're not human, court says
item.description: DENVER (AP) — Five elephants at a Colorado zoo may be “majestic” but, since they're not human, they do not have the legal right to pursue their release, Colorado’s highest court said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN SLEVIN, Associated Press, ABC 4
